articles.title: Jake Paul's Betr Brings Polymarket To One Million Sports Bettors
articles.summary: Jake Paul's sports betting platform Betr is partnering with Polymarket to expose its one million users to prediction markets beyond traditional sports wagers. This marks the biggest mainstream crossover yet for crypto prediction markets, potentially bringing serious liquidity and legitimacy to political and event betting that has mostly lived in DeFi corners.

articles.title: BETR TO LAUNCH PREDICTION MARKETS IN PARTNERSHIP WITH POLYMARKET
articles.summary: BETR is partnering with Polymarket to launch its own prediction markets platform, bringing together the sports betting company's user base with Polymarket's political and news betting infrastructure. The move signals prediction markets are expanding beyond crypto-native platforms into mainstream betting apps, potentially exposing millions of traditional sports bettors to political and event wagering for the first time.

articles.title: Prediction Markets Are Eroding Our Civic Soul
articles.summary: Someone's arguing that prediction markets are turning civic engagement into a cold, transactional betting game that strips away the moral and emotional investment democracy requires. If this critique gains traction, it could fuel regulatory crackdowns or public backlash that limits where and how political betting operates.

articles.title: How Is Kalshi Not Gambling?
articles.summary: Kalshi has managed to operate as a regulated derivatives exchange while offering markets that look suspiciously like sports betting, raising questions about where exactly the line falls between prediction markets and gambling. Their regulatory gymnastics could determine whether other platforms can follow the same playbook or if regulators will crack down on what they see as betting dressed up in financial market clothing.

articles.title: What sickos are out here betting on the Texas primary elections?
articles.summary: Texas primary elections are drawing action from political betting degenerates who apparently can't wait until November to lose money on democracy. These early-stage wagers signal that prediction markets are expanding beyond major races into hyper-local politics, creating more granular data on voter sentiment but also attracting the kind of bettors who probably have strong opinions about school board meetings.

articles.title: How Anonymous Bettors Cashed In on the Iran Strike, Just Hours Before It Happened
articles.summary: Anonymous traders made massive bets on Middle East escalation just hours before Iran launched its missile strike on Israel, suggesting someone had advance intelligence of the attack. The timing raises serious questions about information leakage in geopolitical prediction markets and whether insiders are using classified intel to game these platforms.

articles.title: A Darkly Modern Guide to Betting on War
articles.summary: War betting markets have exploded in popularity as geopolitical tensions escalate, with traders now wagering on everything from invasion timelines to casualty counts. These morbid markets reveal information that traditional intelligence sources miss, but they're also creating perverse incentives where profits depend on human suffering.

articles.title: What Is Kalshi? The Platform That Made Prediction Markets Legit
articles.summary: Kalshi transformed prediction markets from a legal gray area into a regulated, mainstream betting platform where Americans can legally wager on everything from election outcomes to economic indicators. The platform's CFTC approval legitimized an entire industry and opened the floodgates for institutional money and retail traders who previously had to use offshore sites or sketchy alternatives.

articles.title: You might not be able to buy a Rolex, but you can now bet on the future price of one
articles.summary: A new prediction market has launched that lets traders bet on future Rolex prices, bringing luxury watch speculation to the masses who can't afford the actual timepieces. This opens up a massive new asset class for prediction markets, tapping into the $20B+ luxury watch market where prices swing wildly based on celebrity endorsements, limited releases, and pure hype.

articles.title: US derivatives regulator to advance prediction markets rule soon
articles.summary: The CFTC is preparing to release new regulations governing how prediction markets operate in the US. These rules will determine which platforms can legally offer political and event betting to Americans, potentially opening the floodgates for mainstream adoption or keeping the market locked down to a few licensed players.

articles.title: Kalshi licenses election data from AP
articles.summary: Kalshi signed a deal to license election data directly from the Associated Press, giving the prediction market access to AP's race calls and vote tallies. This moves Kalshi closer to real-time settlement on election bets, potentially cutting hours off payout times compared to competitors who rely on secondary sources.

articles.title: Prediction market bets on Iran strikes stoke insider trading, ethics scrutiny
articles.summary: Prediction markets are facing heat over potential insider trading as bettors place wagers on Iran military strikes, raising questions about whether traders have access to classified information. The scrutiny threatens to bring unwanted regulatory attention to prediction markets just as they're gaining mainstream acceptance, potentially leading to new restrictions on geopolitical betting.
